file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## CRITICAL: AgentRail Workflow (MUST follow)

This project uses AgentRail for structured handoffs between sessions. You MUST run these commands:

**FIRST thing every session** -- before reading code, before doing any work:
```bash
agentrail next
```
Read the output. It contains your plan, current step, prompt, skill docs, and past trajectories.

**LAST thing every session** -- after committing code, before ending:
```bash
agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "tools and approach used" \
  --next-slug <next-step> \
  --next-prompt "instructions for next agent"
```
Use `--reward -1 --failure-mode "reason"` if the step failed.
Use `--done` instead of `--next-*` if the saga is finished.

Do NOT skip these commands. The next agent session depends on your trajectory recording.

## Project

Tiny Macro Lisp (tml24c): a minimal Lisp-1 with lexical scope, unhygienic defmacro, closures, and mark-sweep GC. Compiles to COR24 24-bit RISC assembly.

## Related Projects

- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tc24r` -- Tiny COR24 compiler (Rust)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` -- Coding skills domain

## Available Task Types

`c-project-init`, `c-compile-fix`, `lisp-define-form`, `pre-commit`

## Build

```bash
make          # build
make clean    # clean
```

Compiler flags: `-Wall -Wextra -Werror -std=c11`. Never suppress warnings.

content.text:
Here's the CLAUDE.md contents:

- **Project**: Tiny Macro Lisp (tml24c) — a minimal Lisp-1 with lexical scope, unhygienic defmacro, closures, and mark-sweep GC, targeting COR24 24-bit RISC assembly.
- **AgentRail workflow**: Must run `agentrail next` at session start and `agentrail complete` at session end for structured handoffs.
- **Related projects**: cor24-rs (assembler/emulator), tc24r (compiler), agentrail-domain-coding (skills domain).
- **Build**: `make` / `make clean`, with `-Wall -Wextra -Werror -std=c11` flags — never suppress warnings.
- **Task types**: `c-project-init`, `c-compile-fix`, `lisp-define-form`, `pre-commit`.

message.content: run agentrail next now and complete when done with this "step"

input.description: Run agentrail next to get current step

content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a Tiny Macro Lisp system that compiles to COR24 assembly. Phase 1: lexer and parser. Phase 2: evaluator with core special forms. Phase 3: compiler targeting COR24 assembly. Phase 4: integration with tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain.

=== STEPS ===
  [!] 001-lexer [production]: Implement the Lisp lexer: tokenize parentheses, symbols, numbers, strings, an...
  [x] 002-research-and-plan [production]: Read research, study COR24 toolchain, create architecture/prd/design/plan/stats docs
  [x] 003-scaffold [production]: Set up C source structure based on architecture
  [ ] 003-003-scaffold [legacy]: Set up C source structure: create src/tml.h (Value type, tags, macros, forwar...
  [x] 004-003-scaffold-cor24 [legacy]: Redo scaffold for COR24 target. CC=tc24r, run with cor24-run --run. No libc h...
  [ ] 004-lexer [production]: Implement tokenizer for Lisp syntax
  [ ] 005-reader [production]: Implement S-expression reader <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 005-005-reader [legacy]: Implement S-expression reader in src/read.h. Parse from char* string input. H... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 006-eval-core [production]: Implement eval with core special forms
  [ ] 007-gc [production]: Implement mark-sweep garbage collector
  [ ] 008-compiler [production]: Compile to COR24 assembly
  [ ] 009-integration [production]: Wire to tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain

=== CURRENT STEP: 005-reader [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement S-expression reader
Task type: c-compile-fix

=== SKILL: c-compile-fix (v1) ===
Fix C compiler warnings and errors without disabling warnings

Procedure:
  1. Run make (with -Wall -Wextra -Werror) to see all warnings
  2. Fix each warning by correcting the code, not by suppressing it
  3. NEVER add #pragma GCC diagnostic ignored
  4. NEVER remove -Werror or -Wall from CFLAGS
  5. Re-run make to confirm zero warnings
  6. Run tests if available to confirm fixes did not break anything

Known failure modes:
  - pragma_suppress (4x): Using #pragma to suppress warnings instead of fixing them
  - void_cast (3x): Casting to void* to silence type warnings instead of fixing types

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

file.content:
#pragma once

/* tml.h -- Tiny Macro Lisp core types and macros
 *
 * All values are a single COR24 int (24-bit).
 * Low 2 bits = tag, upper bits = payload.
 *
 *   [23..2] payload  [1:0] tag
 *   00  Fixnum   -- 22-bit signed integer
 *   01  Cons     -- heap cell index
 *   10  Symbol   -- symbol table index
 *   11  Extended -- heap cell index (closure, macro, primitive, string)
 */

/* Tags */
#define TAG_FIXNUM   0
#define TAG_CONS     1
#define TAG_SYMBOL   2
#define TAG_EXTENDED 3
#define TAG_MASK     3

/* Tag checks */
#define IS_FIXNUM(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_FIXNUM)
#define IS_CONS(v)     (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_CONS)
#define IS_SYMBOL(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_SYMBOL)
#define IS_EXTENDED(v) (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_EXTENDED)

/* Fixnum encode/decode */
#define MAKE_FIXNUM(n)  (((n) << 2) | TAG_FIXNUM)
#define FIXNUM_VAL(v)   ((v) >> 2)

/* Pointer (index) encode/decode */
#define MAKE_CONS(idx)     (((idx) << 2) | TAG_CONS)
#define MAKE_SYMBOL(idx)   (((idx) << 2) | TAG_SYMBOL)
#define MAKE_EXTENDED(idx) (((idx) << 2) | TAG_EXTENDED)
#define PTR_IDX(v)         ((v) >> 2)

/* Special values: nil = symbol 0, t = symbol 1 */
#define NIL_VAL   MAKE_SYMBOL(0)
#define T_VAL     MAKE_SYMBOL(1)
#define IS_NIL(v) ((v) == NIL_VAL)

#define HEAP_SIZE 4096

/* Extended object type tags */
#define ETYPE_CLOSURE   MAKE_FIXNUM(1)
#define ETYPE_MACRO     MAKE_FIXNUM(2)
#define ETYPE_PRIMITIVE MAKE_FIXNUM(3)
#define ETYPE_STRING    MAKE_FIXNUM(4)

     5→

     7→
     8→$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h
     9→	@mkdir -p build
    10→	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)
    11→
    12→run: $(ASM)
    13→	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM)
    14→

    17→

file.content:
TC24R = tc24r
COR24_RUN = cor24-run
SRC = src/main.c
ASM = build/tml24c.s

all: $(ASM)

$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h
	@mkdir -p build
	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)

run: $(ASM)
	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM)

clean:
	rm -rf build

content.text: Good — I have the full picture. Now I'll implement the S-expression reader in `src/read.h`. It needs to parse from a `char*` string, handling integers, symbols, lists (proper and dotted), and quote shorthand.

content.text: Compiles clean. Let's run it:
Output was cut at 10s time limit. Let me try with a longer limit:
Hanging on negative number `-7`. Likely a right-shift or negative fixnum issue on COR24. Let me diagnose:

input.description: Test negative fixnum round-trip on COR24

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

Assembled 304 bytes
Running (speed: 100000 IPS, time limit: 10s)...


Executed 830000 instructions

toolUseResult.stdout:
Assembled 304 bytes
Running (speed: 100000 IPS, time limit: 10s)...


Executed 830000 instructions

content.text: Nothing printed at all — issue is fundamental with negative numbers on COR24. Let me narrow down:

content.content:
-7


Executed 370 instructions
CPU halted (self-branch detected)

content.text: OK, basic negative printing works! The issue must be with `<<` or `>>` on negative values. Let me test:

input.description: Test shift operations on negative values

content.content:
UART output: -28


Executed 830000 instructions

content.text: Right shift on negative values is the problem — COR24 uses logical shift. Let me verify:

input.description: Check if right shift is logical on COR24

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

Assembled 336 bytes
Running (speed: 100000 IPS, time limit: 10s)...


Executed 828000 instructions

Running (speed: 100000 IPS, time limit: 10s)...


Executed 828000 instructions

content.text: Right shift itself is hanging. Let me check the generated assembly:

input.description: Test positive vs negative right shift

content.content:
A


Executed 126 instructions
CPU halted (self-branch detected)

toolUseResult.stdout:
A


Executed 126 instructions
CPU halted (self-branch detected)

input.description: Full output including UART TX lines

Assembled 124 bytes

[UART TX @ 126] '\n'

[UART TX @ 126] '\n'

A


Executed 126 instructions

[UART TX @ 126] '\n'
[UART TX @ 126] 'A'  (0x41)
[UART TX @ 126] '\n'

A

content.text: Both work! So `>> 2` on negative values does NOT hang by itself. The hang was in my previous tests probably due to the division in `print_int` with a large positive number (from logical right shift). Let me confirm that `>>` on negative values gives logical (not arithmetic) result:

input.description: Test if right shift is arithmetic or logical

content.content:
UART output: P
Executed 830000 instructions

content.text: Confirmed: COR24 uses logical right shift, so `FIXNUM_VAL` breaks for negative fixnums. The fix is to use division by 4 instead, which preserves sign:

input.description: Test division by 4 as arithmetic right shift alternative

content.content: UART output: 0

content.text: Division on negatives also doesn't work correctly (gives 0). Let me try the conditional OR approach:

input.description: Test conditional OR fix for FIXNUM_VAL

content.content: UART output: -7

[UART TX @ 567] '\n'

UART output: -7
42


Executed 567 instructions
CPU halted (self-branch detected)

toolUseResult.stdout:
[UART TX @ 567] '\n'

UART output: -7
42


Executed 567 instructions
CPU halted (self-branch detected)

content.text: Both `-7` and `42` correct. Now I need to make `FIXNUM_VAL` a function instead of a macro. Let me also check if `0xC00000` literal works or if I need a different approach — it clearly worked. Let me update `tml.h`:

file.content:
#pragma once

/* tml.h -- Tiny Macro Lisp core types and macros
 *
 * All values are a single COR24 int (24-bit).
 * Low 2 bits = tag, upper bits = payload.
 *
 *   [23..2] payload  [1:0] tag
 *   00  Fixnum   -- 22-bit signed integer
 *   01  Cons     -- heap cell index
 *   10  Symbol   -- symbol table index
 *   11  Extended -- heap cell index (closure, macro, primitive, string)
 */

/* Tags */
#define TAG_FIXNUM   0
#define TAG_CONS     1
#define TAG_SYMBOL   2
#define TAG_EXTENDED 3
#define TAG_MASK     3

/* Tag checks */
#define IS_FIXNUM(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_FIXNUM)
#define IS_CONS(v)     (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_CONS)
#define IS_SYMBOL(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_SYMBOL)
#define IS_EXTENDED(v) (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_EXTENDED)

/* Fixnum encode/decode */
#define MAKE_FIXNUM(n)  (((n) << 2) | TAG_FIXNUM)
#define FIXNUM_VAL(v)   ((v) >> 2)

/* Pointer (index) encode/decode */
#define MAKE_CONS(idx)     (((idx) << 2) | TAG_CONS)
#define MAKE_SYMBOL(idx)   (((idx) << 2) | TAG_SYMBOL)
#define MAKE_EXTENDED(idx) (((idx) << 2) | TAG_EXTENDED)
#define PTR_IDX(v)         ((v) >> 2)

/* Special values: nil = symbol 0, t = symbol 1 */
#define NIL_VAL   MAKE_SYMBOL(0)
#define T_VAL     MAKE_SYMBOL(1)
#define IS_NIL(v) ((v) == NIL_VAL)

#define HEAP_SIZE 4096

/* Extended object type tags */
#define ETYPE_CLOSURE   MAKE_FIXNUM(1)
#define ETYPE_MACRO     MAKE_FIXNUM(2)
#define ETYPE_PRIMITIVE MAKE_FIXNUM(3)
#define ETYPE_STRING    MAKE_FIXNUM(4)

 *

 * Low 2 bits = tag, upper bits = payload.

 */

/* Tags */
#define TAG_FIXNUM   0
#define TAG_CONS     1
#define TAG_SYMBOL   2
#define TAG_EXTENDED 3
#define TAG_MASK     3

/* Tag checks */
#define IS_FIXNUM(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_FIXNUM)
#define IS_CONS(v)     (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_CONS)
#define IS_SYMBOL(v)   (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_SYMBOL)
#define IS_EXTENDED(v) (((v) & TAG_MASK) == TAG_EXTENDED)

/* Fixnum encode/decode */
#define MAKE_FIXNUM(n)  (((n) << 2) | TAG_FIXNUM)
#define FIXNUM_VAL(v)   ((v) >> 2)

/* Pointer (index) encode/decode */
#define MAKE_CONS(idx)     (((idx) << 2) | TAG_CONS)
#define MAKE_SYMBOL(idx)   (((idx) << 2) | TAG_SYMBOL)
#define MAKE_EXTENDED(idx) (((idx) << 2) | TAG_EXTENDED)
#define PTR_IDX(v)         ((v) >> 2)

/* Special values: nil = symbol 0, t = symbol 1 */
#define NIL_VAL   MAKE_SYMBOL(0)
#define T_VAL     MAKE_SYMBOL(1)
#define IS_NIL(v) ((v) == NIL_VAL)

#define HEAP_SIZE 4096

/* Extended object type tags */
#define ETYPE_CLOSURE   MAKE_FIXNUM(1)
#define ETYPE_MACRO     MAKE_FIXNUM(2)
#define ETYPE_PRIMITIVE MAKE_FIXNUM(3)
#define ETYPE_STRING    MAKE_FIXNUM(4)

content.text:
All tests pass. Every reader test case produces correct output:

```
scaffold ok
42 => 42 [42]
-7 => -7 [-7]
0 => 0 [0]
foo => foo [foo]
nil => nil [nil]
+ => + [+]
() => nil [nil]
(1 2 3) => (1 2 3) [(1 2 3)]
(1 . 2) => (1 . 2) [(1 . 2)]
(a (b c)) => (a (b c)) [(a (b c))]
'x => (quote x) [(quote x)]
(+ 1 2) => (+ 1 2) [(+ 1 2)]
(define x 42) => (define x 42) [(define x 42)]
reader ok
```

What was done:
1. **`src/read.h`** — S-expression reader parsing integers, symbols, proper/dotted lists, and `'` quote shorthand
2. **`src/tml.h`** — Fixed `FIXNUM_VAL` for COR24's logical right shift (sign-extension via `| 0xC00000`)
3. **`src/main.c`** — Added reader test suite with 12 test cases
4. **`Makefile`** — Added `read.h` dependency

Now completing the agentrail step:
